question: Which directories and files in a standard Astro project have special meanings, and where should pages, components, public assets, and source assets be placed?
How are Astro components structured, how do they receive props, and how can named and default slots compose child content?
How does Astro turn files in src/pages into routes, and which page file types can generate HTML or endpoint responses?
What are client islands and server islands in Astro, and how do they differ in where and when interactive or personalized content is rendered?
How do Astro Actions define validated server operations, return data and errors, accept HTML form submissions, and enforce authorization?
How are Astro sessions configured and accessed from pages, endpoints, actions, and middleware?
How does Astro middleware use context, locals, chaining, and rewrites to affect a request and response?
How do server islands defer rendering, display fallback content, transfer props, and interact with caching and deployment encryption keys?
How do build-time and live content collections differ in configuration, loaders, query APIs, execution time, and storage behavior?
What image workflows does Astro provide for local, public, remote, Markdown, MDX, and content-collection images?
How is internationalized routing configured in Astro, including locale prefixes, fallback behavior, custom locale paths, and domains?
How does Astro resolve static, dynamic, rest-parameter, redirect, rewrite, and reserved routes, and what priority order applies when routes conflict?
How do Astro static file endpoints and on-demand server endpoints define methods, access params and requests, and return responses?
How are environment variables loaded and accessed in Astro, and how can astro:env schemas enforce type, context, and access constraints?
How does Astro's ClientRouter perform client-side navigation, preserve state, control transitions, and expose lifecycle events?
What TypeScript support and configuration does Astro provide, and how should component props, imported types, and strictness presets be handled?
How do Astro projects import local files, npm packages, aliases, JSON, and other assets?
How does Astro render Markdown content, expose frontmatter and headings, configure remark and rehype plugins, and mix Markdown with MDX?
Which major behaviors can astro.config control for output, adapters, routing, build paths, images, i18n, security, sessions, and integrations?
What do Astro's common, client, server, script, and style directives do, and which constraints apply to their values?
How should content collections and Astro routing be combined to generate static collection pages versus request-time dynamic collection routes?
How can actions, sessions, and middleware cooperate on authenticated form handling while keeping per-action authorization explicit?
How should an Astro content collection represent and render local or remote images while preserving image optimization and correct dimensions?
How do i18n route configuration, route rendering mode, and astro:i18n URL helpers work together when locale domains are enabled?
What must be configured to add a server island to an otherwise static page, and how do the adapter, server:defer directive, and request-time features divide responsibility?
How should client-side scripts be authored when ClientRouter is enabled so initialization runs at the correct lifecycle point without accidental duplication?
When should an Astro route be a page, a static endpoint, or a server endpoint, and how do filename parameters and rendering mode affect each choice?
How should a deployment provide typed runtime secrets to request-time code and a session driver without unintentionally inlining replaceable configuration at build time?
How do pages, components, and layouts compose a reusable Astro site, and where do props and slots cross each boundary?
How do output mode, an adapter, per-route prerender settings, and deployment-provider support determine whether an Astro project can serve static and request-time routes together?

A member-only settings form calls an Astro Action and may rewrite unauthenticated submissions to a login page. Design the authorization, session, and rewrite boundaries so an attacker cannot bypass permissions and an HTML form body is not consumed by an unsafe middleware rewrite.

A mostly static product page uses a cacheable personalized server island behind a CDN during rolling multi-region deployments. Derive the minimum adapter, prop, URL, request-size, caching, and encryption-key rules needed to keep the island correct and cacheable.

An Astro storefront has versioned MDX product descriptions and optimized local images, but inventory and price must be fresh on every request. Design the collection split, configuration files, loaders, query APIs, rendering mode, and error behavior without pretending both data classes have the same lifecycle.

A catalog mixes imported images from src, passthrough files in public, and remote CMS images whose dimensions are not known. Derive a secure image policy that states which assets Astro processes, when dimensions are mandatory or inferable, and which remote URLs may be optimized.

An Astro site maps locales to different production domains and needs helpers to generate absolute localized links. Derive the required output, site, adapter, and prerender configuration, and explain the proxy-header and unsupported-adapter failure modes.

A server-rendered Astro application needs a private API token at request time and a Redis-backed session driver whose host can change after build. Design the environment schema and access points, then explain which configuration locations would inline or expose the values incorrectly.

A project contains a literal /products/new page, a prerendered /products/[id] page, an on-demand /products/[...slug] page, a redirect, and an API endpoint that can overlap. Determine route priority and state when getStaticPaths is required versus when params can be read directly at request time.

A static marketing page needs one interactive framework widget and one personalized account panel. Decide which should be a client island and which a server island, including hydration directives, SSR behavior, serializable props, fallback UI, adapter requirements, and the no-JavaScript default.

After enabling ClientRouter, a page initializer fires twice on some navigations and not at all on others. Derive a script-loading contract covering processed module deduplication, inline scripts, data-astro-rerun, lifecycle events, and guards for persistent global listeners.

A primarily static Astro site needs one cookie-aware account page and one request-time API route, while all other routes should stay prerendered. Derive the output, adapter, per-route, endpoint, and request-feature configuration, including the inverse design if server output becomes the default.
